title: Surface incoming LICHEN messages and status to Meshtastic app
description: Why this exists: the MVP requires the app to see incoming LICHEN message/status data. What needs to be done: add an adapter-facing event API for incoming LICHEN local messages/status updates, translate those events into Meshtastic FromRadio/protobuf messages, queue them over BLE, and handle disconnected clients. Acceptance criteria: tests inject a LICHEN message/status event and verify the encoded app-visible response; queue limits and drop policy are deterministic; disconnected clients do not lose adapter state unsafely; app display behavior is smoke-testable.
notes:
2026-07-01 partial no-hardware implementation slice: added adapter-facing incoming text primitive lichen_meshtastic_adapter_emit_text(), public incoming text event struct, shared text payload limit, Meshtastic MeshPacket/Data text encoder, and FromRadio wrapping. Behavior covered: explicit payload validation including UTF-8 and 200-byte limit; disconnected sessions return -ENOTCONN without mutation; missing enqueue op returns -ENOTSUP; queue backpressure is deterministic; generated FromRadio/MeshPacket ids advance only after successful enqueue; stats.incoming_text_count advances only on success.

Follow-up blockers created and linked because the full t2hn.8 acceptance also requires status events, production gateway event injection, and app-visible smoke harness: project-LICHEN-t2hn.8.1 incoming status/ROUTING_APP events; project-LICHEN-t2hn.8.2 wire real LICHEN inbound message/status events into the gateway adapter path; project-LICHEN-t2hn.8.3 no-hardware app-visibility smoke harness. Parent t2hn.8 remains in_progress/blocked by these child Beads and should not close until they pass.

Reviews: initial reviews found P2 codec coverage gap, P2 real BLE queue full-policy coverage gap, and P1 acceptance gaps for status/gateway/smoke behavior. P2 gaps fixed in this slice; P1 acceptance gaps filed as child blockers. Post-fix reviews: Singer no remaining codec P0/P1/P2; Socrates no remaining adapter P0/P1/P2; Kepler no remaining test/Beads P0/P1/P2.

Verification: git diff --check PASS. Local qemu_x86 lichen/tests/meshtastic_codec PASS 19/19; RAM 180280 B / 31 MB (0.55%). Local qemu_x86 lichen/tests/meshtastic_adapter PASS 38/38; RAM 204856 B / 31 MB (0.63%). Local qemu_x86 lichen/apps/gateway build PASS; RAM 235676 B / 31 MB (0.73%). Local qemu_x86 BLE run attempted on wrong board and failed because /tmp/bt-server-bredr was absent; corrected to native_sim. Local native_sim build is Linux-only and failed on macOS with Zephyr POSIX architecture error, so EC2 was used per project rule. EC2 i-09d16b45c46a69785 in us-east-2a with EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 mounted at /mnt/lichen-zephyr; primary EBS worktree was dirty/behind, so validation used clean detached worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-t2hn8-clean based on origin/main be3ddc35 plus this slice's source/test files. EC2 native_sim lichen/tests/meshtastic_ble PASS 8/8, including test_full_from_radio_queue_rejects_new_packet. Native_sim emitted expected warning: Bluetooth device missing; tests do not require a physical/virtual HCI device.
close_reason: All children closed (status events, gateway wiring, smoke harness). Tests pass on EC2 native_sim (8/8 BLE, 38/38 adapter, 19/19 codec). Incoming text/status events encode to FromRadio and enqueue over BLE.
